subject: Mathematics
title: Chinese Counting Rods
description: A 60-second lesson on Chinese Counting Rods.

hook.text: While Europe used Roman numerals, China had a place-value system with bamboo rods.

buildup.text: Rod numerals used horizontal and vertical sticks to represent digits in alternating columns.

discovery.text: This system included a blank space for zero, centuries before India formalized the concept.

twist.text: Chinese mathematicians also discovered negative numbers using red and black rods.

climax.text: By 100 BC, China had tools for algebra that Europe wouldn't develop for another millennium.

punchline.text: Bamboo rods held mathematical secrets a thousand years early.

quiz.question: What advantage did Chinese counting rods have over Roman numerals?
options: They were easier to carve in stone
